Assault on Nayeem Hasan by PoliceThe IncidentOn the night of Friday, June 12, 2026, cricketer Nayeem Hasan arrived in Chattogram from Dhaka after participating in a match. While returning home from the airport via a CNG-run autorickshaw, his vehicle was stopped by police at the toll plaza area of the Lalkhan Bazar flyover in Chattogram city.Assault and HarassmentNayeem Hasan alleged that despite identifying himself, Sub-Inspector (SI) Shafiqul Islam of Khulshi Police Station, Constable Russell, and a police source named Sohel dragged him out of the autorickshaw and assaulted him with sticks and pipes. He was then taken to the police station, where he faced further harassment. He was eventually released following the intervention of Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) officials.Legal ActionFollowing the incident, Nayeem Hasan’s brother, Sabbir Hasan, filed a case at the Khulshi Police Station, naming SI Shafiqul, Constable Russell, and the police source, Sohel, as defendants.Administrative MeasuresIn response to the incident, SI Shafiqul and Constable Russell were initially withdrawn (closed) to the police lines and subsequently suspended from duty. Additionally, the police source, Sohel, has been arrested. The Chattogram Metropolitan Police (CMP) has formed a three-member investigation committee to probe the matter.Overall SituationThe assault on Nayeem Hasan has sparked deep concern and widespread condemnation from the Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB), the cricketers' association, and various other stakeholders.
